name: Toa Luau
tagline: The only luau on Oahu with a perfect 5.0 Google rating — intimate, authentic, and set inside Waimea Valley's lush botanical garden.
hours: Check-in 3:30pm–4pm, show runs until ~8pm. Schedule varies seasonally
bestTime: Book at least 2 weeks ahead — they sell out. Arrive early to walk Waimea Valley before the luau starts
parking: Free parking at Waimea Valley entrance, 59-864 Kamehameha Hwy
talkStory: What sets Toa Luau apart from the mega-luaus is scale. Max 200 guests (vs 800+ at the big ones), set under a canopy of trees inside Waimea Valley. The Polynesian performance is intimate enough that you can see the dancers' expressions. Run by a Samoan family with deep roots in Polynesian culture — the fire knife dance finale is genuinely thrilling, not a phoned-in tourist show. The buffet includes kalua pig from an actual imu (underground oven) dug that morning, plus poi, laulau, and haupia. Your ticket includes same-day Waimea Valley admission, so arrive at 2pm to hike to the waterfall first. A perfect 5.0 rating across 4,500+ reviews is almost unheard of — this is the real deal.
mustOrder: kalua pig straight from the imu
arrive early and swim at Waimea Falls with your included admission
